ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the weather station */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the weather station */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the weather station */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the weather station */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the station */,
  "station_status" TEXT /* Current status of the weather station (e.g., active, inactive, maintenance) */,
  "station_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the weather station */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the weather station was installed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_codes" (
  "code_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather code */,
  "code_name" TEXT /* Code name (e.g., RA, FZFG) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the weather code */,
  "code_category" TEXT /* Category of the weather code (e.g., precipitation, temperature, wind) */,
  "code_subcategory" TEXT /* Subcategory of the weather code */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("code_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_data_codes" (
  "data_code_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data-code relationship */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data entry */,
  "code_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather code */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_code_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_codes_code_id FOREIGN KEY ("code_id") REFERENCES weather_codes ("code_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., meteorologist, data analyst, admin) */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the user role */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data entry being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_duration" REAL /* Duration of the access event in seconds */,
  "access_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_files" (
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data file */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data entry the file belongs to */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the data file */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the data file in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the file was uploaded */,
  "file_format" TEXT /* Format of the data file (e.g., CSV, JSON) */,
  "file_checksum" TEXT /* Checksum value for data integrity verification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("file_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alert_rules" (
  "rule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert rule */,
  "rule_name" TEXT /* Name of the alert rule */,
  "condition" TEXT /* Condition for triggering the alert */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message to be sent when the alert is triggered */,
  "rule_severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert rule (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "rule_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the alert rule */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rule_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alert_logs" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert log */,
  "rule_id" INTEGER /* ID of the alert rule that was triggered */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data entry that triggered the alert */,
  "trigger_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was triggered */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message of the alert */,
  "alert_acknowledged" INTEGER /* Whether the alert has been acknowledged by a user */,
  "alert_response" TEXT /* User response to the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alert_logs_rule_id FOREIGN KEY ("rule_id") REFERENCES alert_rules ("rule_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_quality" (
  "quality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality metric */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data entry */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the quality metric (e.g., accuracy, completeness) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the quality metric */,
  "metric_unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the quality metric */,
  "metric_threshold" REAL /* Acceptable threshold value for the quality metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("quality_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the data source (e.g., satellite, ground-based) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "source_format" TEXT /* Format of the data provided by the source (e.g., CSV, JSON) */,
  "source_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which data is updated from the source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_source_integration" (
  "integration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data-source relationship */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data entry */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("integration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_source_integration_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_visualizations" (
  "viz_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data visualization */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data entry used for the visualization */,
  "viz_type" TEXT /* Type of the visualization (e.g., chart, map) */,
  "viz_path" TEXT /* File path to the visualization */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the visualization */,
  "viz_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the visualization */,
  "viz_format" TEXT /* Format of the visualization (e.g., image, interactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("viz_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data report */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data entry used for the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of the report (e.g., daily, monthly) */,
  "report_path" TEXT /* File path to the report */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the report */,
  "report_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the report */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, HTML) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id")
)
